<a id="class-instanceplaceholder"></a>

# InstancePlaceholder

**Inherits:** [Node](class_node.md#class-node) **<** [Object](class_object.md#class-object)

Placeholder for the root [Node](class_node.md#class-node) of a [PackedScene](class_packedscene.md#class-packedscene).

## Description

Turning on the option **Load As Placeholder** for an instantiated scene in the editor causes it to be replaced by an **InstancePlaceholder** when running the game, this will not replace the node in the editor. This makes it possible to delay actually loading the scene until calling [create_instance()](#class-instanceplaceholder-method-create-instance). This is useful to avoid loading large scenes all at once by loading parts of it selectively.

**Note:** Like [Node](class_node.md#class-node), **InstancePlaceholder** does not have a transform. This causes any child nodes to be positioned relatively to the [Viewport](class_viewport.md#class-viewport) origin, rather than their parent as displayed in the editor. Replacing the placeholder with a scene with a transform will transform children relatively to their parent again.

## Methods

| [Node](class_node.md#class-node)                   | [create_instance](#class-instanceplaceholder-method-create-instance)(replace: [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) = false, custom_scene: [PackedScene](class_packedscene.md#class-packedscene) = null)   |
|----------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| [String](class_string.md#class-string)             | [get_instance_path](#class-instanceplaceholder-method-get-instance-path)()                                                                                                                            |
| [Dictionary](class_dictionary.md#class-dictionary) | [get_stored_values](#class-instanceplaceholder-method-get-stored-values)(with_order: [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) = false)                                                                        |

---

## Method Descriptions

<a id="class-instanceplaceholder-method-create-instance"></a>

[Node](class_node.md#class-node) **create_instance**(replace: [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) = false, custom_scene: [PackedScene](class_packedscene.md#class-packedscene) = null) [🔗](#class-instanceplaceholder-method-create-instance)

Call this method to actually load in the node. The created node will be placed as a sibling *above* the **InstancePlaceholder** in the scene tree. The [Node](class_node.md#class-node)'s reference is also returned for convenience.

**Note:** [create_instance()](#class-instanceplaceholder-method-create-instance) is not thread-safe. Use [Object.call_deferred()](class_object.md#class-object-method-call-deferred) if calling from a thread.

---

<a id="class-instanceplaceholder-method-get-instance-path"></a>

[String](class_string.md#class-string) **get_instance_path**()  [🔗](#class-instanceplaceholder-method-get-instance-path)

Gets the path to the [PackedScene](class_packedscene.md#class-packedscene) resource file that is loaded by default when calling [create_instance()](#class-instanceplaceholder-method-create-instance). Not thread-safe. Use [Object.call_deferred()](class_object.md#class-object-method-call-deferred) if calling from a thread.

---

<a id="class-instanceplaceholder-method-get-stored-values"></a>

[Dictionary](class_dictionary.md#class-dictionary) **get_stored_values**(with_order: [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) = false) [🔗](#class-instanceplaceholder-method-get-stored-values)

Returns the list of properties that will be applied to the node when [create_instance()](#class-instanceplaceholder-method-create-instance) is called.

If `with_order` is `true`, a key named `.order` (note the leading period) is added to the dictionary. This `.order` key is an [Array](class_array.md#class-array) of [String](class_string.md#class-string) property names specifying the order in which properties will be applied (with index 0 being the first).
